statement: A 15 kg block is pulled up a frictionless incline angled at 30° from horizontal by a cable parallel to the surface. The block accelerates up the incline at 2 m/s². Find the cable tension T and the normal force N. Use g = 9.81 m/s².
common_student_errors: Using the full weight (mg) along the incline instead of the component mg·sin(30°)
Setting up the equation as T = ma without accounting for the gravity component along the slope
Confusing the normal force component: using mg instead of mg·cos(30°)
